entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: Semi-arid tropics (SAT) region, spread over 11.6 million km2 worldwide, characterized by low fertility soils and water stress

entities.name: LOW FERTILITY
entities.description: Condition of soils in the semi-arid tropics, often depleted of macro, micro, and secondary nutrients

entities.name: WATER STRESS
entities.description: Condition for crop growth in the SAT caused by undependable rainfall and high average temperatures

entities.name: GREEN REVOLUTION
entities.description: Agricultural transformation in Asia that largely bypassed rainfed systems in the semi-arid tropics

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: International center committed to improving livelihoods in the SAT through agricultural research

entities.name: WATERSHED MODEL
entities.description: Management approach emphasizing water as an entry point for improving livelihoods through natural resource-based activities

entities.name: ZINC
entities.description: Micronutrient found to be deficient beyond critical limits in SAT soils

entities.name: BORON
entities.description: Micronutrient found to be deficient beyond critical limits in SAT soils

entities.name: SULFUR
entities.description: Secondary nutrient found to be deficient beyond critical limits in SAT soils

entities.name: ANDHRA PRADESH
entities.description: State in India where widespread deficiencies of micro and secondary nutrients were observed

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: Country where the research on nutrient amendments in SAT was conducted

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: Agricultural productivity that showed substantial increases (20-120%) due to nutrient amendments

entities.name: NITROGEN
entities.description: Macronutrient whose adequate amendment, combined with micronutrients, further increased crop yields

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS
entities.description: Macronutrient whose adequate amendment, combined with micronutrients, further increased crop yields

entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: Crop that showed yield increases due to micronutrient and macronutrient amendments

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Crop that showed yield increases due to micronutrient and macronutrient amendments

entities.name: MUNG BEAN
entities.description: Crop that showed yield increases due to micronutrient and macronutrient amendments

entities.name: PIGEON PEA
entities.description: Crop that showed yield increases due to micronutrient and macronutrient amendments

entities.name: CASTOR
entities.description: Crop that showed yield increases due to micronutrient and macronutrient amendments

entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: Crop that showed yield increases due to micronutrient and macronutrient amendments

entities.name: LAND DEGRADATION
entities.description: Environmental condition minimized by the use of nutrient amendments and natural resource management

entities.name: NATURAL RESOURCE MANAGEMENT
entities.description: Interventions integrated with improved genotypes to harness benefits in the watershed

entities.name: INTEGRATED GENETIC AND NATURAL RESOURCE MANAGEMENT
entities.description: Approach combining genetic and natural resource management to realize the Grey to Green Revolution

relationships.description: Soils in the semi-arid tropics are low in fertility
Climate in the semi-arid tropics creates water stress situations for crop growth
Andhra Pradesh is a region within the semi-arid tropics of India
Andhra Pradesh is a state located in India
Zinc is a micronutrient depleted in the soils of the semi-arid tropics
Boron is a micronutrient depleted in the soils of the semi-arid tropics
Sulfur is a secondary nutrient depleted in the soils of the semi-arid tropics
Application of zinc as a micronutrient amendment increases crop yields
Application of boron as a micronutrient amendment increases crop yields
Application of sulfur as a nutrient amendment increases crop yields
Adequate nitrogen amendment increases crop yields
Adequate phosphorus amendment increases crop yields
Yield increases were observed in maize crops
Yield increases were observed in sorghum crops
Yield increases were observed in mung bean crops
Yield increases were observed in pigeon pea crops
Yield increases were observed in castor crops
Yield increases were observed in chickpea crops
Natural resource management interventions help in minimizing land degradation

relationships.target: INTEGRATED GENETIC AND NATURAL RESOURCE MANAGEMENT
relationships.description: Natural resource management is a core component of the integrated genetic and natural resource management approach

relationships.source: INTEGRATED GENETIC AND NATURAL RESOURCE MANAGEMENT
relationships.description: The IGNRM approach aims to make the Grey to Green Revolution a reality
ICRISAT undertakes agricultural research to improve livelihoods in the semi-arid tropics
